title: About
text: Hello! My name is Jazlynn and I am a student at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln. This is my blog for my senior year coding class. Go Big Red!!!!!

title: How Nebraska Softball Wins Games
text:
When the Nebraska Cornhuskers softball team takes the field, the outcome often comes down to a simple question: can they outscore their opponent? While softball can be influenced by pitching, defense, and momentum, the numbers suggest a clear pattern…Nebraska wins when the runs start piling up. By breaking down scoring and results, we can see just how much offense drives success for this team.


Code
library(tidyverse)


── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
✔ dplyr     1.2.0     ✔ readr     2.1.6
✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
✔ ggplot2   4.0.2     ✔ tibble    3.3.1
✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.2
✔ purrr     1.2.1     
── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors




Code
library(readr)
sfb_pitching <- read_csv("~/Desktop/YouveGotThis!/softballdatablog/posts/husker-softball/data/2026_ncaa_sfb_pitching.csv")


Rows: 308 Columns: 26

dbl (22): IP, HA, R, ER, BB, SO, SHO, 2B-A, 3B-A, HR-A, WP, HB, KL, IBB, Inh...
num  (2): BF, P-OAB

ℹ Specify the column types or set `show_col_types = FALSE` to quiet this message.

Code
big_pitching <- sfb_pitching |>
  filter(Conference == "Big Ten")

neb_pitching <- sfb_pitching |>
  filter(Institution == "Nebraska")




Code
library(dplyr)




Code
library(ggplot2)




Code
neb_avg <- neb_pitching |>
  summarise(avg_runs_allowed = mean(R, na.rm = TRUE))




Code
big_avg <- big_pitching |>
  summarise(avg_runs_allowed = mean(R, na.rm = TRUE))




Code
bar_data <- tibble(
  Team = c("Nebraska", "Big Ten Avg"),
  Runs_Allowed = c(neb_avg$avg_runs_allowed, big_avg$avg_runs_allowed)
)




Code
ggplot(bar_data, aes(x = Team, y = Runs_Allowed, fill = Team)) +
  geom_col() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Nebraska" = "red", "Big Ten Avg" = "lightgrey")) +
  labs(
    title = "Runs Allowed vs Big Ten Average",
    subtitle = "How does Nebraska compare to the rest of the B10",
    x = "",
    y = "Average Runs Allowed",
    caption="Source: NCAA Stats | Credit: Jazlynn S.",
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "none", 
        plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10))









After comparing Nebraska to the Big Ten in terms of average pitching performance with Strikeouts vs Runs Allowed, we can take a closer look at how Nebraska’s runs allowed specifically stack up against the conference average in the bar chart below.


Code
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)

big_pitching <- sfb_pitching |>
  filter(Conference == "Big Ten")

team_avg <- big_pitching |>
  group_by(Institution) |>
  summarise(
    avg_SO = mean(SO, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_R  = mean(R, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

neb_avg <- team_avg |>
  filter(Institution == "Nebraska")

ggplot(team_avg, aes(x = avg_SO, y = avg_R)) +
  geom_point(color = "lightgrey", size = 3) +               # All B10 teams
  geom_point(data = neb_avg, aes(x = avg_SO, y = avg_R), 
             color = "red", size = 4) +                     # Nebraska
  labs(
    title = "Average Strikeouts vs Runs Allowed (Big Ten Teams)",
    subtitle = "Nebraska highlighted in red",
    x = "Average Strikeouts (SO)",
    y = "Average Runs Allowed (R)",
    caption = "Source: NCAA Stats | Credit: Jazlynn S."
  ) +
  theme_minimal() + theme(legend.position = "none", 
        plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10))

dplyr::filter(sfb_pitching, Conference == "Big Ten")

Next we will be looking at Nebraska’s Strikeouts, Walks and Hits


Code
neb_pitch_summary <- neb_pitching |>
  summarise(
    Strikeouts = sum(SO, na.rm = TRUE),
    Walks = sum(BB, na.rm = TRUE),
    Hits = sum(HA, na.rm = TRUE)
  ) |>
  pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "Outcome", values_to = "Count")

ggplot(neb_pitch_summary, aes(x = "", y = Count, fill = Outcome)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", width = 1) +
  coord_polar(theta = "y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
    "Strikeouts" = "lightgreen",
    "Walks" = "red",
    "Hits" = "lightblue"
  )) +
  labs(
    title = "Nebraska Softball Pitching Outcomes (2026 Season)",
    subtitle = "Strikeouts, Walks, and Hits",
    caption = "Source: NCAA Stats | Credit: Jazlynn S."
  ) +
  theme_void() +
theme(legend.position = "right", 
        plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold"),plot.subtitle = element_text(size=10))
